zh.lifeSummary: 當Giles de Clifford於1190年出生時，他的父親Walter de Clifford II42歲，母親Agnes Condet30歲。他於1244年與Eva de Blanchminster在英國賀福爾克利福德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1276年在英國戴文斯托凱特尼黑阿德享年86歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Giles de Clifford于1190年出生时，他的父亲Walter de Clifford II42岁，母亲Agnes Condet30岁。他于1244年与Eva de Blanchminster在英国賀福爾克利福德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1276年在英国戴文斯托凱特尼黑阿德享年86岁。

nameMeanings.name: Clifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Coleman
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

nameMeanings.name: Giles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Much altered English vernacular form of the Late Latin name Aegidius, from Greek Aigidios (a derivative of aigidion ‘kid, young goat’), via Old French Gide. It was the name of an 8th‐century saint who, according to tradition, was an Athenian citizen who fled to Provence because he could not cope with the fame and adulation caused by his power to work miracles, in particular by healing the lame and crippled. This was not a particularly common name in the Middle Ages and its use seems to have been confined to certain families and localities.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Giles de Clifford was born in 1190, his father, Walter de Clifford II, was 42 and his mother, Agnes Condet, was 30. He married Eva de Blanchminster in 1244, in Clifford, Herefordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1276, in Stokeinteignhead, Devon, England, at the age of 86.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Giles de Clifford est né en 1190, son père, Walter de Clifford II, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Agnes Condet, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Eva de Blanchminster en 1244, à Clifford, Herefordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1276, à Stokeinteignhead, Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 86 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Giles de Clifford 1190 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Walter de Clifford II 42 und seine Mutter Agnes Condet 30. Er hat 1244 in Clifford, Herefordshire, England Eva de Blanchminster geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1276 im Alter von 86 Jahren in Stokeinteignhead, Devon, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Giles de Clifford è nato il nel 1190, il suo padre, Walter de Clifford II, aveva 42 anni e il suo madre, Agnes Condet, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Eva de Blanchminster nel 1244, nel Clifford, Herefordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1276, nel Stokeinteignhead, Devon, England, all'età di 86 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Giles de Cliffordが1190年に生まれた時，父Walter de Clifford IIは42歳，母Agnes Condetは30歳であった。1244年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアクリフォードでEva de Blanchminsterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1276年にイギリスデボンストケインテイグンヘドで86歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Giles de Clifford(이)가 1190년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Walter de Clifford II(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Condet(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1244년 영국 허퍼드실르 클리퍼드에서 Eva de Blanchminster(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1276년 영국 데폰 스토케인테익헤드에서 86세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Giles de Clifford nasceu em 1190, seu pai, Walter de Clifford II, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Condet, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Eva de Blanchminster em 1244, em Clifford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1276, em Stokeinteignhead, Devon, Inglaterra, com 86 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Giles de Clifford родился в 1190 г., его отцу по имени Walter de Clifford II было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes Condet, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Eva de Blanchminster в 1244 г. в местности клиффорд, хирефордшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1276 г. в местности стокинтиинид, девон, Англия в возрасте 86 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Giles de Clifford nació en 1190, su papá, Walter de Clifford II, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Agnes Condet, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Eva de Blanchminster en 1244, en Clifford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1276, en Stokeinteignhead, Devon, Inglaterra, a la edad de 86 años.
